element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Family missions establish baseline ship handling before combat introduction
Teaches multi-crew craft mechanics with AI crew support before player combat responsibility
Graduated from transport operations to fighter-class combat roles
Players learn to chain weapons together during initial combat encounters
Reconnaissance missions uncover advanced threats, escalating challenge
Players navigate between regions, adding strategic layer beyond single-zone combat
Culminating threat; connections to Return of the Jedi events
Players learn to manage repairs and rearming between engagement segments

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Missions with capital ships like Millennium Falcon
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players swap between pilot and gunner roles with AI support
Escalates spatial complexity and enemy density significantly from predecessors

typical_relief_triggers: Mail sequences between missions providing story context and character moments
typical_tension_triggers: Large-scale battle initiation with 50+ enemy ships
Family crisis events (death of Tomaas, brother killed, Viraxo competition)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating combat missions with narrative interludes; linear progression from family smuggling operations through escalating Rebel Alliance combat to climactic Battle of Endor; tension steadily climbs through mid-campaign with brief relief via mail/story sequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-driven family missions with forgiving transport operations; establishes baseline piloting mechanics in safe context
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Graduation to fighter combat with moderate threat density; introduction of multi-part missions requiring navigation between hyperjump zones; experimental Imperial fighters raise stakes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Large-scale battles with up to 96 simultaneous ships; Second Death Star operations; climactic Battle of Endor; connections to film events signal narrative and mechanical maturity
notable_spikes: Transition from safe family missions to active Rebel combat
Empire's raid on family station (narrative spike affecting stakes)
Second Death Star discovery and operations

notable_level_patterns.description: Tutorial-style missions where family members and Emkay instruct Ace in Corellian transport operations; establishes lore, family dynamics, and baseline controls in safe environment
Mid-campaign pattern where Ace balances Rebel military assignments with family business and Viraxo competition; creates dual objective structure
Missions spanning multiple combat zones requiring hyperjump transitions between regions; adds navigation and strategic layer beyond single-arena combat
Missions flying or supporting multi-crew vessels like Millennium Falcon; player selects pilot or turret roles with AI crew automation; introduces crew management mechanics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Millennium Falcon and Dash Rendar missions

notable_level_patterns.description: Battles featuring 50-96 simultaneous ships creating complex spatial awareness challenges; scales significantly beyond prior X-Wing titles
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Battle of Endor, Death Star operations
